Issue: apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_ crashes with PyTorch 2.10 due to multi_tensor_applier attempting to access raw data pointer of gradient tensors, which no longer expose traditional storage in PyTorch 2.10
Decision: Upgrade apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_ to be compatible with PyTorch 2.10's tensor storage changes
Rationale: PyTorch 2.10 introduced changes where some tensors (e.g., those with lazy/functional storage) no longer expose traditional storage, causing apex's multi_tensor_applier to fail when accessing raw data pointers of gradient tensors. This necessitates an update to apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_ to handle these new tensor storage behaviors.
Author: KumoLiu
Description: apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_ function in the MONAI repository
Risk: Technical debt: Incompatibility with PyTorch 2.10's tensor storage changes may lead to crashes in gradient clipping operations. Potential side effects include failures in training pipelines that rely on apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_.

Argument.author: KumoLiu
Argument.argument: PyTorch 2.10's changes to tensor storage (removing traditional storage for some tensors) cause apex.contrib.clip_grad.clip_grad_norm_ to crash when accessing raw data pointers, necessitating an update to apex to handle these new behaviors.
